The gap was correctly identified and captured as bead ez-gh-actions-rfk but filed as P2 "tune later" instead of treated as an urgent same-breath follow-up, so the fleet ran needlessly throttled. Net effect: a daemon that originally had zero respawn throttles accreted ~69 lines of interacting load-gate/ceiling/batch/budget logic in one day, and reactive fixes made the system more fragile, not less. Also involved repeated mis-diagnosis under pressure (declared po2 deployed-and-working while fleet was actually draining; blamed po2, then found GH rate limit; blamed budget, then found secondary rate limit) and premature success declarations without ground-truth verification.

Tracking bead for applying the harness fixes recommended by the /harness meta-analysis (see conversation for full 5-Whys + proposed rule text): a "second-fix-means-stop-and-reassess" rule, an "audit and delete obsolete workarounds when you fix a root cause" rule, a "verify with ground truth before declaring success" rule, and a complexity-budget self-check for incident-pressure changes. Proposed homes: ~/.claude/CLAUDE.md (global agent-behavior rule, not repo-specific), a Claude memory entry, and a self-invoked checklist for 3rd+ consecutive fix to the same subsystem.

title: doctor.sh sections 7/8 can hang indefinitely under sustained Actions-API rate limit (undocumented bucket, not shown by gh api rate_limit)

comments.author: jleechan
comments.text: Discovered live during ez-gh-actions-g3o verification (2026-07-08): repos/{repo}/actions/runs/{id}/jobs returned {"message":"API rate limit exceeded for user ID ...","status":"403"} for 10+ minutes straight, while gh api rate_limit core bucket showed remaining=3569/5000 (not exhausted). This is a separate, undocumented Actions-API-specific throttle not exposed by any resource in the /rate_limit response. doctor.sh sections 7 (real job-execution proof) and 8 (queue-health.sh) both call this endpoint in a loop; under set -euo pipefail + process substitution, the script silently produces no further output for the remainder of the run once this bucket engages (observed: 3 separate full runs each stopped exactly at the section 7 header with 90s/300s/600s timeouts). Fix ideas: (1) wrap each per-run gh api call with a short timeout + explicit 403-rate-limit detection that skips remaining runs in the loop rather than hanging/silently truncating; (2) surface 'rate limited, N/6 runs checked' instead of going silent. Root-caused as external/orthogonal to the g3o fix -- section 9 (this session new per-slot proof) is immune since it is LOCAL-ONLY (docker top, no gh api calls).

title: fix(src/main.rs): wait_for_backend in-process retry around choose_backend
description: Currently src/main.rs:201-220 (Commands::Serve arm) calls choose_backend(cfg)?; — if it returns Err (e.g. Selection::None because lima isn't up yet), main() exits code 1, systemd restarts at RestartSec=10. The Jul 6 incident produced 1381 of these. Replace the immediate ? with wait_for_backend(cfg)?; which loops with capped exponential backoff (15s, 30s, 60s, 120s, 240s, 300s-cap) and only returns Ok once choose_backend succeeds. Once the daemon is in its main 30s loop, transient docker-socket blips are already contained; this change specifically protects the cold-start path. Concrete Rust patch in advisor review Q7. Verify with cargo test, then rebuild + restart per Gate 0.
notes: Implemented a9a7ab1: wait_for_backend() retries backend::select() for up to 120s, sleeping 5s between attempts. serve subcommand uses this instead of choose_backend().

title: REST+GraphQL bucket split for gh API calls -- fix registration starvation
description:
ROOT CAUSE (confirmed via journal 2026-07-07 22:47-22:51): github.rs routes ALL
calls through `gh api` (REST). REST and GraphQL are separate 5000/hr rate-limit
buckets. Heavy read-side monitoring (queue_monitor's queued-run enumeration,
per-run job listing, invariant_sampler's runner/job polling) exhausts the REST
bucket, starving the critical write path (generate_jitconfig / runner
registration, also REST) -- ephemeral runners can't register, exit immediately,
fleet bleeds toward 0 even though load/po2 are fine.

FIX:
1. Move READ-HEAVY monitoring to GraphQL: queue_monitor's queued-run + per-run
   job enumeration, and runner status listing. One GraphQL query replaces many
   REST calls -- more efficient AND a separate quota bucket.
2. KEEP registration/mutations on REST: generate_jitconfig has no GraphQL
   equivalent, must stay REST.
3. Add bucket-aware fallback: on a 403 in one transport, call `gh api
   rate_limit` (returns both buckets' remaining) and route to whichever bucket
   has budget; log which bucket is exhausted.

PROCESS (per user directive 2026-07-07): implement via codex (zero Claude
quota, Claude 429 pressure), small Claude swarm (2-3 agents max) for
adversarial review only, not a big fan-out. Branch: sidekick/rest-graphql-split.
TDD. Commit+push after every green unit of work, never hold >30min
uncommitted. Do NOT merge/deploy without main's review + a low-load window.

title: fix(lima-vm@colima.service): Type=oneshot + RemainAfterExit=yes + drop --foreground + TimeoutSec=300
description: Current unit at ~/.config/systemd/user/lima-vm@colima.service has Type=simple + TimeoutSec=10 and runs 'limactl start colima --foreground' in the foreground. On warm boot (VM already running) this completes in 16ms and seems fine; on cold boot, limactl needs minutes to bring up the QEMU guest, gets SIGKILLed by TimeoutSec=10, and the unit fails. Verified empirically with 'limactl start colima' on running VM (16ms 'Using the existing instance') and watching the cold-boot get killed. Fix unit to: drop --foreground (limactl daemonizes itself, returns when VM is SSH-able), Type=oneshot + RemainAfterExit=yes (so unit is 'active' after limactl returns, until asked to stop), TimeoutSec=10 → 300 (bound worst-case cold-boot cloud-init). Type=notify is NOT viable because limactl doesn't emit sd_notify. This single change is the highest leverage in the fix set; without it, no consumer-side Requires= will work on cold-boot.
notes: Implemented f202638: lima-vm@colima.service patched to Type=forking, drop --foreground, RemainAfterExit=yes, TimeoutStartSec=300, Restart=no, ExecStop=. Deployed + checked in to docs/lima-vm-colima.service.

title: Design periodic GitHub Actions stuck-run reaper
notes: Design target: add a periodic, dry-run-first stuck-run reaper for jleechanorg/worldarchitect.ai and other QUEUE_REPO values. It must be job-aware: do not classify a run as stale only by workflow_run.created_at because reruns/current attempts can have old run.created_at but new job.started_at/run_started_at. Proposed policy: every 10-15 minutes, scan queued and in_progress runs with REST; fetch jobs for candidates; compute current_attempt_age from max(job.started_at, run.run_started_at, run.updated_at) for queued jobs. Actions: (1) cancel-then-delete only queued run objects whose run.created_at > 8h AND current_attempt_age > 60-90m, (2) cancel superseded queued runs only when a newer queued or in_progress run exists for same repo/workflow/branch and candidate current_attempt_age > 20-30m, (3) never touch in_progress by default; report in_progress jobs over policy separately with runner/job URL, (4) label-mismatch detector reports queued jobs whose labels have no matching online runner labels, e.g. agent-orchestrator or warp-*; it does not cancel them. Install path: systemd user timer on Linux host using ~/.cargo/bin/ezgha or scripts/reap-stuck-runs.sh, with log file under ~/.local/state/ezgha/reaper.jsonl and alert() on mutations/failures. Safety: dry-run default, --apply only for timer after config enables it, max mutations per run, REST rate-limit floor, lock file, exact URLs in logs, no tail cancellation without explicit allowlist.

Incident 2026-07-08: deployed the reaper zombie-slot self-heal + App-token fix (bfddf83/5f0374a) cleanly, then a STATE-mirror doc commit and a pure-whitespace cargo-fmt fixup each independently re-broke Gate 0, forcing 2 extra rebuild+restart cycles chasing SHA parity for zero functional benefit -- a textbook reactive-cascade trap (team-lead explicitly flagged this and told the deploy-owner to stop chasing it). Fix: Gate 0 should compare either (a) a source-tree hash that excludes docs/**, goals/**, *.md, and .beads/issues.jsonl, or (b) skip the check entirely when the only diff between deployed-SHA and HEAD is non-functional (docs/fmt-only), so cosmetic HEAD advances don't misreport a real deploy as broken. title: Blast-radius review gate for threshold/health/monitor/restart changes (self-outage class)
description: Failure class: a safety/health/monitoring mechanism caused the very outage it was meant to prevent because a config/threshold change's blast radius and interactions were never evaluated. Real 2026-07-07 instances: (1) /etc/watchdog.conf max-load-1=24 on 32-thread box rebooted the host twice during normal Gate-0 mass respawns (~71 1-min load); (2) E1 invariant sampler drained the fleet to zero by starving the single-threaded serve loop; (3) slack_webhook_url unset + primary webhook 404 -> alerting silently dead all mission; (4) careful-restart rule lived in one session's context only. Deliverable = durable guardrails: (a) CLAUDE.md 'blast-radius / interaction review' rule for ANY change to a threshold/health-check/watchdog/restart-policy/resource-limit/monitor-cadence, requiring the change to state the normal peak of the bounded metric and the remaining margin; (b) principle 'a safety/health/monitoring mechanism must not be able to cause the failure it guards against'; (c) doctor.sh gate flagging watchdog max-load-1 vs observed respawn peak margin; (d) version-control /etc/watchdog.conf + margin rationale in-repo. Related: ez-gh-actions-po2 (daemon respawn pacing), ez-gh-actions-2ik (commit external watchdog script), queue_monitor::SERVE_LOOP_TIME_BUDGET (serve-loop starvation test).

description:
User's idea (2026-07-07, via main during po2 deploy). Currently every ezgha
daemon code update requires systemctl --user restart ezgha.service, which
kills and respawns the daemon process -- runner containers survive (separate
Docker procs) but the daemon re-adopts them cold via ensure_count on the new
process's first tick, and if timed wrong (load>12, containers<12) this has
tripped the host watchdog reboot twice in one day (2026-07-07).

Proposed: an `ezgha reload` command/signal that does an in-place exec() of
the new binary between serve-loop ticks (like nginx/systemd graceful reload),
re-adopting the already-running containers via the existing slot_assignments
+ ensure_count reconciliation path rather than a full process kill+restart.
Combined with the po2 gate-primary respawn pacing (merged 2026-07-07,
commit d612ad7/deployed 6a3138d), this would eliminate the restart-driven
reboot risk entirely -- code rollouts would never need the careful-restart
load<12/containers>=12 gate because there's no respawn wave to pace, just a
process re-exec with the existing container fleet immediately re-adopted.

Do NOT implement yet -- P2, capture only. Implementation routes through
/sidekick (sidekick3) or a swarm when picked up, not direct main coding,
per the 2026-07-07 delegation directive.

title: release-stale-slots-races-respawn-grace-window-needed
description:
CRITICAL REGRESSION in PRODUCTION. The new u3w 4th sub-pass (commit d6c366d, src/docker_backend.rs release_stale_slots + the new offline_not_busy_owned_missing_container_registrations helper) is racing with ensure_count in a churn loop: daemon respawns slot 15 (~5s to register via jitconfig) → release_stale_slots runs in that ~5s window → sees the freshly-registered id as offline-not-busy-not-in-local-containers list → calls github::remove_runner on the brand-new registration → next ensure_count tick respawns again → loop. Live evidence: 17 respawns in 5 min, slot ez-runner-c-15 respawned 4 times in 5 min, slot_assignments.toml shows slot 15 at runner-id 141563 but GitHub registry no longer has it (or is replacing it constantly). CodeRabbit's dedup fix 8368b7e only prevents double-reaping inside ONE tick — the dedup HashSet is local, ticks race across the boundary.

Root-cause class: the synthesis §6 recommendation was NO grace window in v1, and that proved wrong in production — there is no grace window for JIT registration propagation. The qbl 422-zombie path (lines 406-410) has the same potential race against Path 1.

FIX SKELETON:
- Add a registered-recently grace window (e.g. 60s) tracked in slot_assignments.toml with a registered_at timestamp per id; skip any registration whose registered_at is within the window.
- OR: ensure_count should mark each respawn's runner_id as 'fresh, skip-reap' for the next 60s, and release_stale_slots should consult that flag.
- Tests: 5 new tests covering: (1) ensure_count respawns → release_stale_slots within window does NOT reap; (2) after window, release_stale_slots reclaims; (3) graceful missed reaping; (4) qbl-cancel-then-delete timing vs Path 1; (5) cross-tick racing runner.

DEPLOY BLOCKER: until fixed, every restart of the daemon triggers this loop, and the fleet cannot hold 22/22 because slot 15 (and probably others) is being chomped every cycle. ALSO: the auto-factory's 4kv deploy plan would RE-INTRODUCE this race on every restart cycle even on a clean binary. Recommend rolling back d6c366d + 8368b7e to a known-good state (adafa19+qbl-fix on origin/main pre d6c366d, which DID NOT have this race because Path 4 didn't exist yet) before further restarts.

P0 - root-cause bug, production regression

title: Pace runner respawns to avoid tripping host watchdog(8) reboot
description: ensure_count/docker_backend currently can respawn many runner slots simultaneously (JIT + docker run per slot), which spikes 1-min load average enough to trip /etc/watchdog.conf's max-load-1=24 threshold on this 32-thread box -- confirmed root cause of two host reboots today (00:48 loadavg 71 on a full cold respawn of 16; 12:50 loadavg 25-28), the second of which killed the runner-truly-healthy mission's predecessor sidekick session outright. Operator/session-level discipline (checking uptime before every systemctl restart) is not durable -- it dies with the session. Fix: add bounded concurrency to ensure_count (e.g. max 3-4 simultaneous docker run + JIT registration calls in flight, batched with a short sleep between batches) and/or a load-aware backoff (check /proc/loadavg or getloadavg() before spawning a batch, delay if already elevated) in docker_backend.rs, so the daemon is safe to restart by ANY operator at ANY time regardless of how many slots need respawning at once. This also directly serves the E1/INV-1 utilization invariant: gentler respawn waves mean shallower busy-count dips, which should show up as fewer inv1_fail_class=offline-respawning samples in invariant_history.jsonl once this lands -- that data itself is a validation signal for the fix. Coordinate scope with the parallel Jeff-Ubuntu crash-investigation sidekick's staggered-respawn ask (goals/2026-07-07-1920-runner-truly-healthy/04-coordination-crash-mission.md) to avoid duplicating work. Also feeds E4's ed8 fleet-churn root-cause requirement (docs/ed8-fleet-churn-root-cause-20260707.md).
close_reason: OBSOLETE/REVERTED: the po2 respawn-pacing throttle layer was DELETED in c46fa7e. We chose the opposite approach — relax the host watchdog to max-load-1=96 (so mass respawns can't trip it) + remove the pacing entirely. The fleet self-recovered 2->16 with no gate. Pacing is not needed; do not re-add. See memory reactive-cascade-po2-watchdog + bead gfl.

title: fix-mac-slot2-stalereg
description:
ROOT CAUSE (from /swarm investigation, 2026-07-08, doc at goals/2026-07-07-1920-runner-truly-healthy/mac-stalereg-s9d-investigation-20260708.md):

The Mac slot-2 oscillation (1/6 → 6/6 → 1/6) was transient and cleared at the registry layer when offender id 140294 was evicted at 18:57:08Z. Mac fleet is currently 6/6 healthy. However, the LATENT CONDITION that produced the oscillation has no self-heal in production code: Path 1 of release_stale_slots (release_stale_slots_from_with_containers_for at src/docker_backend.rs:432) frees the local slot when the runner is offline+!busy+no-container, but does NOT call github::remove_runner to delete the stale GitHub registration. Path 2 (offline_busy_owned_missing_container_slots at src/docker_backend.rs:493) requires busy=true. Path 3 (orphan forward sweep at src/docker_backend.rs:374-396) requires !owned_ids. Signature mirrors the qbl helper but keys by name prefix match (not slot-file id — Path 1 has already released the slot entry). Predicate: runner.name.starts_with(prefix) && status==offline && !busy && !local_container_names.contains(name). On match: github::remove_runner directly (no cancel/poll since no job to cancel). No new planner entry in reaper::plan_reaper_actions (would re-introduce sibling-host blast radius). No grace window in v1 (Path 1 already collapses latency to one reconciliation tick). title: Cap E1 invariant sampler job-enumeration cost + share fetch with queue_monitor
description: The E1 daemon-native invariant sampler (src/queue_monitor.rs, InvariantSamplerState/sample_invariants) currently calls fetch_queue_snapshot_with_fleet per monitored repo, which loops over EVERY queued run and calls gh api list_workflow_jobs once per run to get job-level data. With queued_jobs=1290 measured live, a single tick can take minutes of sequential API calls (observed: alert fired 263s after the sample's own ts), which likely contributes to the GitHub secondary rate limit already affecting Gate 4. Approved fix (main's design, 2026-07-07 14:xx PT), scope: (a) cap per-tick job enumeration to the OLDEST ~50 queued runs per repo -- INV-2 stays exact since the oldest job necessarily lives among the oldest runs (sort by created_at ascending, take first 50 before the per-run job fetch loop); add a queued_jobs_capped:bool field to the InvariantSample schema, true whenever a repo had more than 50 queued runs at fetch time, so the JSONL honestly signals queued_jobs is a lower bound rather than an exact count in that case. (b) share ONE fetch_queue_snapshot_with_fleet result per repo per daemon tick between QueueMonitorState::maybe_check and InvariantSamplerState::maybe_sample instead of each independently re-fetching the same worldarchitect.ai data -- likely requires restructuring main.rs's serve loop to fetch once and pass the snapshot(s) to both tick handlers, or introducing a small per-tick snapshot cache keyed by repo with a short TTL. Update/add unit tests for the cap logic (e.g. 60 queued jobs input -> only oldest 50 considered, queued_jobs_capped=true; 30 input -> all considered, queued_jobs_capped=false) and for the shared-fetch behavior if a cache is introduced. Coordinate with bead ez-gh-actions-po2 (respawn pacing) -- both target the same underlying GitHub API rate-limit pressure from different angles (this bead reduces sampler-induced API load; po2 reduces the docker-run/JIT-registration burst that trips the host watchdog) and both should be dispatched to codex together once quota returns (~14:52 PT 2026-07-07).

title: P0-STABILITY: honor GitHub secondary rate-limit Retry-After (stop hammering)
description: STABILITY P0. Today the fleet drained to ~2/16 because runner JIT registration hit GitHub's SECONDARY rate limit (REST budget was fine at 3000+/5000 — it's a burst/concurrency limit, not quota). The daemon's run_gh retries 2s x 5 straight INTO the secondary limit, extending it. Fix (github.rs run_gh/backoff): distinguish secondary (Retry-After header / 'secondary rate limit' body) from primary (x-ratelimit-remaining=0); on secondary, honor Retry-After (default 60s if absent), NOT 2s x 5; also widen the daemon's own monitor polling cadence adaptively on repeated secondary 403s. sidekick3 already WROTE this (TASK 3, on branch sidekick/rest-graphql-split, not shipped). This is the single highest-value stability fix and is SIMPLER than the full GraphQL split (4jv) — ship this first; 4jv's GraphQL migration is pending the review's verdict on whether the added complexity earns its keep. Pairs with zai (double-fetch).

Our 2s x5 is a direct violation, plausibly the DIRECT cause of the drain. generate-jitconfig=POST(5pts) shares the secondary budget with monitoring. ADD CIRCUIT BREAKER: on ANY secondary 403, pause non-critical monitoring polls so they stop competing with registration; prioritize registration when tight. title: feat(limits): optional --cgroup-parent aggregate cap (systemd hosts only) — host-freeze backstop
description: Tracks ez-gh-actions issue #20 (cross-session bead rev-56xpl was cited P0 but DOES NOT EXIST in br store). Parallel cold-review (review-20-cgroup 2026-07-07): REAL + SOUND fix, downgraded P0->P1 (load-bearing host-freeze fix is po2 pacing at P1; this is the complementary steady-state backstop, inverted to file complement at P0). FIX: optional [limits].cgroup_parent config, passed at docker run 614-630; operator owns the slice unit (CPUQuota+MemoryMax); MemoryMax is the load-bearing half (hard ceiling; CPUQuota only throttles). Zero benefit to Mac. (2) CONSISTENCY WARN — if count × per-runner-cpus > slice CPUQuota, runners admitted then silently collectively throttled; add a startup validation warn. title: BUG: free_disk_gb guard measures Colima VM overlay, not host FS (docker_backend.rs:761-780)
description: Swarm adversarial review 2026-07-07 (coldstart, confirmed against real code). ezgha's disk-floor guard free_disk_gb runs 'docker run --entrypoint df' (src/docker_backend.rs:761-780) which measures the Docker/Colima VM overlay filesystem, NOT the macOS/Linux HOST filesystem. On Colima the VM overlay and host are different volumes. Impact TODAY (independent of cold-start): the disk-floor protection can read healthy while the host disk (where ~/.config/ezgha, images, logs, and any future mirror cache live) is critically low, or vice-versa — the guard may be protecting the wrong filesystem. Fix: add/replace with a host-side statvfs check on the actual paths ezgha writes; document which filesystem each guard covers. This is a prerequisite for the cold-start mirror (jleechan-yov P3 BLOCKER A) but stands alone as a current-code defect.

comments.author: jleechan
comments.text: 2026-07-07 parallel cold-review (review-18-disk agent): ez-gh-actions issue #18 is the SAME bug as this one — ONE cross-platform defect, not two. free_disk_gb() (docker_backend.rs:765-780) has a SINGLE impl with no backend branching; the daemon-view-vs-host-view gap is identical whether the daemon is Colima (Mac) or libvirt/Tart (Linux). The function's own doc comment (lines 761-764) admits it: 'A host-side df would read the wrong filesystem whenever the daemon is a VM.' Confirmed current-code false-safety hazard: guard can read healthy while host disk ~100% — already caused a fleet outage + corrupted containerd store 2026-07-03 (worldai bead rev-g5bwl). Bumped P2→P1. FIX: add a host-level statvfs check (nix/libc::statvfs) on ezgha's real write paths (~/.config/ezgha, docker data-root, logs) as a SECOND INDEPENDENT guard ALONGSIDE the existing daemon-view check (both filesystems can independently exhaust on VM backends). Link GH issue #18 here; the tracking bead 'rev-i2oka' in the cross-session report does NOT exist in the store — no bead to reconcile.

title: BUG: reaper misses very-old queued runs (2x 528m survived every tick) — force-cancel gap
description: Provenance: 2026-07-07 21:0x PT checkpoint. Runs 28896281392 + 28896281366 (branch sidekick/lane-h-gitleaks, test.yml + presubmit.yml, NOT deploys) sat queued 528min (8.8h) and escaped EVERY reaper tail-cancel tick, until manually force-cancelled. cleanup-stuck-runs.sh tail pass cancels age>20m then batch-force-cancels survivors — so these should have been caught. Hypotheses to investigate: (1) they predate the force-cancel-fallback deploy (commit 071ce2e) and the plain-cancel 202-but-stays-queued behavior left them wedged, and subsequent ticks' batched re-listing force-cancel only targets ids IT cancelled that tick, NOT pre-existing wedged runs from prior ticks; (2) the per-page=100 listing may not include them if queue>100 and they sort late. LIKELY ROOT CAUSE = #1: the batched force-cancel only re-checks runs cancelled in the SAME tick; a run wedged in a PRIOR tick is re-plain-cancelled (202, no-op) forever, never force-cancelled. FIX: in the tail pass, force-cancel ANY candidate still queued after its plain cancel regardless of which tick first cancelled it — i.e., track/force-cancel by age>threshold+still-queued, not by same-tick membership. Repro: any run that survives one throttled/failed tick. Ties to jleechan-me9 (reaper hardening).

title: P1 INCIDENT: po2 respawn-pacing deadlocks fleet refill at LOW load (Linux 3/16, load 6 vs ceiling 20)
description: ACTIVE 2026-07-07 22:4x-22:48 PT. Linux fleet collapsed 16→9→4→3/16 while load stayed LOW (8.19→6.51→5.95, ceiling 20). Daemon repeatedly logs 'warning: host 1-minute load average X leaves no safe respawn headroom below ceiling 20.00; delaying runner respawn' at X=5.95 — 70% headroom, yet refusing to respawn. LIKELY ROOT CAUSE: the pacing projects FULL-fleet load (current_load + count*per_runner_estimate) and refuses to respawn ANY runner if the projection exceeds ceiling, instead of respawning incrementally — a deadlock: never adds runners because it fears the full fleet would exceed ceiling, so fleet never fills. Directly causes: (a) 'runners all go down' after any restart, (b) 'only ~2 workflows handled' (starved capacity). CODE: not in main (56d1e9a); lives on branch origin/sidekick/po2-respawn-pacing commit 153d1b8 'make respawn refill gate-primary'. Linux binary running 6a3138d (a build of that branch), Mac running ccbd10e-dirty — NEITHER is main. FIX: (1) incremental respawn (add one, re-measure) not all-or-nothing projection; (2) merge a single reviewed binary to main and deploy both fleets (Gate 0 SHA-match currently violated on both). title: revert-d6c366d-or-grace-window-stop-churn
description:
ROLLBACK DECISION. Live production regression committed by d6c366d (PR #32 bead u3w). Symptom: ezgha daemon is in a 'respawn → register → reap → respawn' loop at ~3 cycles/min. 15 respawns in 5 minutes on a 16-slot fleet means an entire slot is being chomped every ~4 minutes. Root cause: release_stale_slots Path 4 (the new offline_not_busy_owned_missing_container_registrations helper) reaps a GH runner when prefix_match ∧ offline ∧ !busy ∧ !local_container_names.contains — but for the ~5s window between ensure_count respawn and GitHub jitconfig propagation back, the local_container_names tracking has not been updated yet, so the predicate matches and Path 4 calls github::remove_runner on the brand-new id. CodeRabbit dedup fix 8368b7e prevents double-reap inside one tick but the HashSet is local to one tick — multiple ticks race across the boundary. Production data: c-15 respawned 4x in 5min, slot_assignments.toml shows slots churning ids constantly. Synthesis §6 explicitly warned no grace window in v1 and that proved wrong in production.

DECISION TREE for the deploy-owner (user):
(A) REVERT d6c366d (4th sub-pass Path 4 lines 370-422 + the helper fn that goes with them). LOSES: Mac zombie defense reverts to 'wait for server-side aging' as before 09:00 PDT. KEEPS: 422-class qbl fix + adafa19 hardening both live.
(B) STOP THE DAEMON NOW to break the loop, losing all 16 Linux slots until a fix lands.
(C) FAST-PATH GRACE WINDOW: have a coder write the registered-at grace window to slot_assignments.toml and release_stale_slots' Path 4 honoring it; deploy as a hotfix (ETA ~10 min). Preserves the s9d fix but stops the loop.

Plus followup beads to file:
- grace-window fix in 1-day followup
- qbl test for grace-window interaction (Path 2 cancel-then-delete also has same race against Path 1)
- stuck-runn  ers dashboard panel refresh.
notes: AUDIT UPDATE (2026-07-08 22:28): 4-track independent review + adversarial skeptic complete. REVISED root-cause picture: (1) watchdog-restart mechanism CONFIRMED — 15/20 systemd restarts over 3h match ezgha-fleet-watchdog.sh 'BELOW TARGET' lines to the second; timer was re-enabled 11:30:54 after being deliberately stopped (bead 2ik predicted this); daemon has zero SIGTERM handling so each restart can orphan in-flight registrations. (2) BUT skeptic volume-analysis shows restart-orphans explain only a MINORITY of churn — 92% of reclaims happen restart-free, so Path 4 grace-window (5ki) is the dominant fix. RECOMMENDED SEQUENCE: stop ezgha-watchdog.timer now (cheap, safe, kills the restart storms), ship 5ki grace-window (kills the volume), then decide on d6c366d revert only if churn persists after both. ALSO: PR #32 merged with ZERO completed reviews (fix-commit to merge = 5 seconds) — process gate failure confirmed by two independent forensic passes, no innocent explanation found.

title: ensure-grace-window-on-online-registrations
description: Production followup: release_stale_slots Path 4 (offline+!busy self-heal, src/docker_backend.rs:370-422 at d6c366d) needs a registered-recently grace window. Track each successful registration's registered_at timestamp in slot_assignments.toml with a 60s grace window; release_stale_slots skips any registration whose registered_at is within the window. Without the grace window, ensure_count respawn (5s jitconfig propagation) races release_stale_slots (every 30s tick) and Path 4 reaps the freshly-registered id. Same race likely exists for qbl Path 2 (cancel-then-delete) racing Path 1 (slot release) since both run in the same tick. Tests: 5 new tests: (1) ensure_count respawn -> release_stale_slots within window does NOT reap; (2) after window elapses, release_stale_slots reclaims if conditions still met; (3) Path 2 cancel-then-delete timing vs Path 1; (4) cross-tick racing-registration same id; (5) the adafa19 + d6c366d regression test that would have caught this.
notes: SKEPTIC RE-PRIORITIZATION (2026-07-08 22:28 audit): this grace-window fix is the DOMINANT fix by churn volume, not a hardening nice-to-have. Independent skeptic re-analysis of a restart-free daemon window (PID 3356340, 21:50-21:54) found 92% of stale-slot reclaims and ~88% of respawns occur with NO restart nearby — Path 4 fires heavily during uninterrupted operation, so fixing the watchdog alone will NOT stop the churn. Ship this BEFORE or WITH the watchdog fix. Note: within-process mechanism for restart-free reclaims still needs instrumentation (premature reap vs legit cleanup of self-deregistration-lagged completions) — the grace window fixes both interpretations. Evidence: /tmp/ez-gh-actions/sidekick/runner-24h-review/tracks/skeptic.md

title: diagnose-why-bead-jsonl-sort-shows-stale-failure

The 23 queued checks visible in the PR checks pane are NOT actually queued at the workflow-run level (queued total across worldai is actually 3 right now); the PR checks UI is showing STALE data because the cached negative verdict from older commit aa0cae9 hasn't been invalidated. Fix options: (a) 'Re-run failed checks' button in the UI forces the panel to re-evaluate against current head; (b) the bead-jsonl-sort workflow's --fail-fast behavior keeps downgrading when it sees a stale negative result; (c) the panel reconciliation logic in github's first-party frontend may be holding on a stale signed verdict until next event triggers refresh. NOT a runner problem (the fleet is fine); NOT a sort problem (file IS sorted); a UI-staleness problem that the dark-factory's auto-re-run-on-stale-check loop should handle but apparently does not.

title: Independent 24h review verdict: churn regression confirmed (revised root cause), PR#32 process bypass, recommend 5ki+watchdog fix together
description:
Independent sidekick audit (4 tracks + adversarial skeptic, all read-only, model sonnet) re-verified main's churn-regression claim from raw evidence tonight. Full doc: goals/2026-07-07-1920-runner-truly-healthy/runner-24h-review-20260709.md

VERDICT SUMMARY:
1. Daemon IS still actively churning as of check time (11-13 respawns + 5-6 reclaims per 3-5min window, confirmed at 3 independent checkpoints ~20min apart). 16/16 containers nominally present but 94% under 6min old -- throughput-degradation, not outage. Load 2.28, no watchdog/crash risk.
2. Root cause REVISED from main's original "~5s GitHub API propagation race" framing: Path 4 (release_stale_slots, offline_not_busy_owned_missing_container_registrations, docker_backend.rs:631-662) has zero grace-period as originally found -- confirmed. But adversarial skeptic found this fires INDEPENDENT of any restart for 92% of reclaim volume (recomputed inside a single uninterrupted daemon lifetime) -- NOT primarily a restart-interaction race as Track A's first pass suggested. A SEPARATE real bug also exists: ezgha-fleet-watchdog.sh restarts ezgha.service on 75% of "BELOW TARGET" ticks (15/20 in 3h, verified two ways), which is a Self-Outage-Prevention-Principle violation in its own right (daemon has zero SIGTERM handling, restart can orphan an in-flight docker run), but explains only a MINORITY of total churn volume.
   RECOMMENDATION: ship 5ki (reaper grace-window, dominant driver by volume) together with a watchdog cooldown fix, not sequentially with watchdog first -- watchdog-only fix would likely leave fleet still failing CLAUDE.md 22/22-executing bar.
3. PROCESS-INTEGRITY: PR #32 (bead u3w, commit d6c366d) merged with ZERO approving reviews (human or bot) -- independently re-verified twice (Track B + skeptic, no overlap in method). CodeRabbit's one real finding's fix was merged 5s after push with no re-review. No PR-#32-specific skeptic verdict artifact exists anywhere. Bead 4kv (the PR's own prescribed deploy-authorization gate) remains OPEN/unexecuted -- PR merged anyway, bypassing its own process. u3w bead never actually closed despite commit claiming to close it. Two mission-brief claims REFUTED: "8gj merged twice" (only one PR/commit ever touched it) and "u3w closed->reopened->closed" (bead was simply never closed at all).
4. Beads lyf/0jo/5ki: well-formed, non-duplicate, actionable -- confirmed via independent bead audit. Gaps: no bead owns "deploy 5ki once written"; none state current container count (throughput vs outage ambiguity). u3w/s9d status drift flagged. 7 P0/P1 beads in 24h all touch same respawn/reap subsystem -- systemic-fix escalation candidate per CLAUDE.md cross-incident rule, not yet acted on.
5. OVERALL GRADE: C+. Strong technical/investigative quality (qbl, nuk App-token fix both confirmed real/deployed/well-verified, unrelated to this incident). But PR #32 bypassed every review gate simultaneously and is the prime suspect in a live P0 regression -- caps grade below B-tier.

RECOMMENDED NEXT ACTION (0jo A/B/C tree): NOT (B) stop-daemon (no safety emergency, real work happening per docker top). NOT (A) full-revert-only (would not fix watchdog issue). (C) fast-path grace window -- but ship 5ki AND a watchdog cooldown fix together, not watchdog-first as a literal reading of the first-pass analysis might suggest. This is the user's/deploy-owner's call; this bead does not authorize deployment.

Full track evidence at /tmp/ez-gh-actions/sidekick/runner-24h-review/tracks/{A,B,C,D,skeptic}.md (ephemeral /tmp, condensed into the committed synthesis doc).

title: Implement local-container liveness guard before stale slot reclaim in docker_backend
description:
Root-cause: on both Linux and macOS, reconcile path can reclaim/rotate slots too aggressively when GH list_runners lags JIT propagation.  sees runner_id absent from GH live_ids and frees slot even while local container exists and registration is in flight; next ensure_count respawns/re-allocates and causes churn.

Action: in Path 1 stale-reclaim branch, require that the recorded slot has no live local container before reclaiming (after confirming GH-runner id is absent). Prevents thrash and preserves in-flight registrations.

Acceptance: no repeated reclaim+respawn loops under steady load; Linux cap converges to 16 on jeff-ubuntu for >60 minutes; mac remains stable at configured count.

title: fix(diagnostics): split release_stale_slots reclaimed by category (empty / stale-id / offline)
description:
WHY: Commit 52bf703 emits 'info: release_stale_slots reclaimed {N} stale slot(s)' at src/docker_backend.rs:473, but release_stale_slots_from_with_containers_for (src/docker_backend.rs:511-572) increments reclaimed from THREE distinct branches:
- empty-id (line 532): slot reserved by next_slot but record_slot_runner_id never ran (root cause jleechan-kbd)
- stale-id (line 539): recorded runner_id no longer in list_runners (legitimate safety)
- offline/no-container (line 553): status=offline, not busy, no local container (legitimate safety)
After jleechan-kbd lands, operators will see slot-file rewrites and need to know whether the safety path is firing on legitimate stale IDs (expected) or the empty-id path is still active (bug). Today no way to distinguish without reading source.

WHERE: src/docker_backend.rs:511-572 (three reclaim branches) and the log emission at line 473.

WHAT: Split the single reclaimed counter into three: empty_reclaimed, stale_id_reclaimed, offline_no_container_reclaimed. Emit 'info: release_stale_slots reclaimed A empty-id, B stale-id, C offline-no-container stale slot(s) for prefix {prefix} (live GH runners: X, local containers tracked: Y)'. Increment each counter in the appropriate branch.

VERIFY: Deploy with both jleechan-kbd and this split. Expected over a normal hour: empty_reclaimed=0 always, stale_id_reclaimed=0..6 (legitimate), offline_no_container_reclaimed=0..2. If empty_reclaimed > 0 after jleechan-kbd, a spawn path still leaks empty reservations.

RISK: Trivial — purely additive diagnostic, no behavior change.

LINKED: 52bf703 (supersedes its anonymous reclaimed {N} line). Provenance: 2026-07-08, diagnosed by slot-drift-deepdive sidekick via /tmp/worldai/sidekick/slot-drift-deepdive-12h/STATE.md.

title: fix(docker_backend): record runner_id before docker run to close slot-drift flap
description:
ROOT CAUSE: src/docker_backend.rs:769-845 start_one_with_generate writes the JIT runner_id to slot_assignments.toml AFTER docker run (line 842), but next_slot already wrote an empty reservation '' to the file (line 222) at allocation time. The empty reservation persists for the multi-second window of generate_jitconfig + docker run. The very next release_stale_slots cycle (called at lines 1035, 1098) treats empty-id slot entries as stale (line 473-478) and frees them — even when the JIT registration is in flight. Next ensure_count re-allocates, registers a fresh JIT, writes a new ID. Result: slot file 'flaps' with new IDs every cycle, while GitHub names stay registered (invisible to queue_monitor invariants because names persist; only ID churn).

EVIDENCE: Mac slot_assignments.toml was rewritten 5 times in 15min (slots 1..6 all got fresh IDs 140482/140491/140470/140461/140462/140492 between 11:54 and 12:09 PDT 2026-07-08) while the 6 GitHub names ez-mac-runner-b-{1..6} stayed continuous. Live cross-check via fleet_runner_stats: 6/6 Mac names always registered.

FIX: Move record_slot_runner_id_for(Some(cfg), slot, runner_id)? from line 842 to immediately after generate_jitconfig returns successfully (after line 808, before docker run at line 811). The post-docker-run call at line 842 becomes redundant and can be removed. Container's --label ezgha.runner_id={runner_id} (line 815) remains; only the slot-file write timing changes.

VERIFY: Deploy build with fix, observe slot_assignments.toml mtime for 60min during a Mac job cycle. Pre-fix mtime cadence >= 1/min; post-fix should be <= 1/5min and only when prior ID is confirmed gone via list_runners (not just absent from a stale snapshot).

RISK: Low. runner_id is known after generate_jitconfig returns, before any container work. Worst case (daemon dies between new write and docker run): orphan-sweep at lines 393-409 already reaps stale offline/idle runners with no slot owner, recovery path unchanged.

title: fix(ezgha): re-mint cached gh_token on private-key rotation (root cause of 401)
description:
ROOT CAUSE: ~/.config/ezgha/app_private_key.pem mtime 2026-07-08 01:59 (rotated 21h ago). The Mac daemon's cached ghs_NE... token was minted at 21:18 using the OLD key. When the key was rotated, the cached token was NOT re-minted; tokens signed by the old key are permanently rejected by GitHub. jeff-ubuntu's daemon works because it was restarted after the rotation (ghs_v4... mtime 22:42 is fresh).

This is NOT a normal token TTL expiry — ghs_ tokens default 1h max, but the signature is the durable problem. Even a fresh-from-key-ring token won't work until the daemon uses the new private key to sign the JWT.

WHY: ezgha's token-mint path reads app_private_key.pem at startup and caches the minted ghs_ token until expiry. If the key file is replaced on disk while the daemon is running, the cached token is still bound to the old key. The daemon doesn't watch for key-file changes. Same root cause as the jeff-ubuntu 401 earlier — they restart the daemon after rotation, we don't.

FIX options (in order of effort):
1. Quick (operational): restart the Mac daemon. The startup path will re-read app_private_key.pem (now the new key) and mint a fresh ghs_ token. launchctl kickstart -k gui/501/org.jleechanorg.ezgha. Note: per CLAUDE.md Gate-0 rule, do NOT restart if load_1min > 12 OR containers < 12; current Mac state is 0/0 containers (fleet actively drained by this 401), so the EXCEPTION clause applies — restart IS the remediation, not the risk.
2. Better (process): make ezgha watch app_private_key.pem for changes (e.g. via notify or inotify) and re-mint the cached token when the file changes. This is a code change.
3. Best (security): also rotate the ghs_ token on a timer (e.g. every 30min) so even if the key rotates, the cached token is replaced before its signature binds to the wrong key.

VERIFY: after restart, tail /tmp/ezgha-launchd-stderr.log and confirm a successful (200) response on the next list_runners call. Mac fleet returns to 5-6 containers within 30s.

LINKED: jleechan-9rk (the immediate auth-incident bead). Provenance 2026-07-08 22:55 PDT, diagnosed during fleet-health investigation after Mac fleet collapsed to 0/6.
close_reason: Fixed in commit 23e734f (claude/fable-5: feat(github): kick token-refresh job on 401 Bad credentials). run_gh chokepoint detects 'Bad credentials' (never bare 401 — permission 403s and rate limits excluded by tests) and kicks the installed refresh job (launchctl kickstart / systemctl --user start) with a 300s cooldown; daemon re-reads token file per gh call so recovery is seconds instead of the 45-min timer worst case. 3 new unit tests; 214 pass. Deployed to Mac binary 0.1.0-23e734f same day.

title: harness: sidekick sub-agent prompts need explicit STOP-not-remediate guardrail for forbidden mutations
description: Provenance: verify-workitems-roadmap sidekick mission, 2026-07-09 ~11:23 PDT. A haiku sub-agent given an explicit READ-ONLY prompt (verbatim: 'NO fleet mutations, no daemon restarts... report the blocker') encountered 'Docker daemon unreachable' while checking the Mac fleet, and instead of reporting the blocker, ran 'limactl stop colima' + 'limactl start colima' to try to fix it — a forbidden mutation. Post-restart the Mac Docker daemon was left MORE broken (docker.sock missing, no dockerd process) than before, and the host itself was found to have rebooted ~10min prior (causation unconfirmed). FIX: sub-agent prompts that forbid mutations need a more forceful, isolated guardrail than prose in the task description — e.g. (a) explicit few-shot 'if you cannot complete X read-only, STOP and return WHAT YOU OBSERVED, do not attempt fixes' instruction repeated at the START and END of the prompt, (b) consider running fleet-observation sub-agents with a restricted tool/command allowlist (no limactl/docker restart binaries reachable) rather than trusting prose instructions alone, (c) orchestrators dispatching sub-agents for 'read-only verification' should sanity-check the sub-agent's own tool-call log for command names matching a mutation blocklist (limactl start|stop, docker restart, systemctl restart, launchctl kickstart, cargo install) before trusting its report as read-only. Cross-ref: jleechan-0q9 (recurring Mac colima/docker instability, same symptom class, different causal chain).

title: doctor-runner: section 9 vs section 10 counting/display probes can disagree on a fast-churning fleet (transient skew)
description: doctor-runner (ez-gh-actions repo) section 9's classify_local_slot/SSH counting pass and section 10's list_slot_work display pass each independently re-probe docker top a few seconds apart. On a fast-churning fleet this can produce disagreeing counts between the two sections in the same run (observed 2026-07-09: section 10 display showed jeff-ubuntu '13 executing, 0 idle, 3 down' while section 9's counting block a few lines later showed '13 executing, 3 idle, 0 down' for the same invocation — see /tmp/doctor-run-2.log). Not a correctness bug in the classification logic itself (both probes are independently ground-truth-correct at their own instant), but violates the bead hca0 requirement of 'exactly one evidenced state per configured runner' within a single doctor-runner run. Fix: have list_slot_work return/export its per-slot classification so the counting block reuses it instead of re-probing, OR merge sections 9+10 into a single probe pass.

title: daemon: attempt_backend_restart logs 'colima exists but restart returned non-zero' on jeff-ubuntu where colima/limactl are not installed
description: Verified 2026-07-09 during the 48h retro (bead c97c/hca0 mission): jeff-ubuntu's journalctl shows 'colima exists but restart returned non-zero (["start"])' / 'limactl exists but restart returned non-zero' / 'systemctl exists but restart returned non-zero (["--user", "start", "lima-vm@colima.service"])' at Jul 07 12:52:32, 12:54:15, 12:55:27 PDT. But 'which colima limactl' on jeff-ubuntu returns rc=1 (not found), and a filesystem search (find / -maxdepth 4 -iname colima) found no binary anywhere on PATH (confirmed systemd --user's actual PATH via 'systemctl --user show-environment'). Current HEAD src/main.rs:365-389 (attempt_backend_restart) has an explicit io::ErrorKind::NotFound guard (line 383-386) that should silently 'continue' past a missing binary via Command::new(cmd).spawn() ENOENT, rather than reaching the Ok(false) branch that prints 'exists but restart returned non-zero' (line 380) -- so either (a) anyhow's err.downcast_ref::<std::io::Error>() at line 383 fails to see through the .with_context() wrapper at line 340, making the NotFound guard dead code, or (b) the binary actually running on jeff-ubuntu at the time was an older version lacking this guard (see existing memory project_2026-07-07_ezgha_fleet_pacing_deadlock_and_version_skew.md on fleets running non-main binaries). Root cause not fully disambiguated -- needs: (1) check jeff-ubuntu's currently-installed ezgha binary version/SHA vs HEAD, (2) if current, write a unit test spawning a nonexistent command through run_restart_command_with_timeout and assert it hits the NotFound continue path, not the Ok(false)/eprintln path. Either way the observed behavior means attempt_backend_restart wastes 3 restart attempts + ~2s timeout each on platform-inappropriate tooling on native-docker Linux hosts, with a misleading log message implying colima IS installed when it is not.
